summary: Obesity exerts mechanical stress on mesenteric structures, potentially triggering deformations visible on multidetector computed tomography as 'misty mesentery'. This suggests a plausible causal relationship wherein metabolic factors associated with excess body mass contribute directly to incidental detection of this finding during imaging studies.
